Web Development

Mobile App Development

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Time Period: July 11, 2026 — July 18, 2026
Introduction: This week has been a highly productive one with multiple parallel tracks yielding fruitful results. We not only deeply integrated AI chat capabilities and overhauled the homepage visuals in “Product Cube”, but also fortified the Apple payment pipeline in the “Seed Backend Platform”. Meanwhile, Easy I18n and “Leaf Note” have also seen significant experience refinements. Here is the detailed iteration log for this week.

📦 Product Cube: AI-Driven Features & Complete Homepage Revamp
This week, “Product Cube” welcomed a major update. It not only significantly enhanced the AI interaction experience but also conducted a comprehensive UI/UX overhaul of the homepage, while smoothly advancing the packaging of v1.3.0 and the preparation for the ProductHunt launch.
📊 Weekly Overview

• Features Delivered: 14

• Content Operations: 3 social media posts
🚀 Core Highlights

• 🤖 Comprehensive Expansion of AI Chat Capabilities:

• Added the ability to “create a project with a single sentence”, significantly lowering the barrier to entry.

• Deeply integrated AI optimization and compatibility, supporting free-form Q&A and chatting regarding “overall project details”, “promotional articles”, and “weekly reports”.

• Optimized the chat interaction and copy for the homepage weekly report, making the AI assistant understand your project better.

• 🎨 Complete UI/UX Revamp of the Homepage:

• Conducted sweeping layout adjustments across the homepage, covering: the weekly report module, weekly active data, heatmap, project list, and dashboard UI.

• Added homepage statistics for per-project active status, and optimized the Level calculation logic and title copy for the heatmap, making data presentation more intuitive.

• 🚀 ProductHunt Launch Preparation:

• Added ProductHunt store information and related optimization logic, gearing up for the upcoming overseas launch.

• ✨ Detail Experience Refinements:

• Added a “tone selection” feature for generating release logs, with simultaneous UI optimizations on the editing page.

• Chat messages now support two copy methods: Markdown and rich text, catering to different scenario needs.

• Language settings are now “saved automatically upon selection”, reducing the number of clicks required from users.
🛠️ Under the Hood

• Complete Removal of Git CLI Dependency: Refactored the underlying logic for fetching Git commit records. It no longer relies on the local Git command-line tool, and fixed errors related to reading time ranges and method signatures, significantly improving cross-platform compatibility.

• Fixed an issue where the version selector did not exclude “completed versions” when editing requirements.

• Merged the feature-v1.2.0 and feature-security branches, completing a smooth transition from v1.1.3 to v1.3.0.

🌱 Seed Backend Service Platform: Payment Fortification & Multi-App Management Upgrade
As the core hub supporting multiple products, the Seed platform focused this week on resolving timing vulnerabilities in the Apple payment pipeline and significantly enhancing the backend experience for multi-app management.
📊 Weekly Overview

• Features Delivered: 9
🚀 Core Highlights
💳 Apple Payment Pipeline Fortification:

• Investigated and fixed timing issues with Apple order callbacks.

• Corrected anomalies in order information timestamp conversion and resolved potential errors in fetching Apple historical orders, ensuring the absolute accuracy of transaction data.
🎨 Backend Management Experience Upgrade:

• Introduced brand new UI styles for the app homepage and list pages, supporting the display of app logos.

• Optimized the configuration modification process: automatically returns to the previous page upon successful modification, and supports “remembering the user’s selected app”.

• App configuration values now fully support multiple languages, and the server home path directly displays the app list.
🌍 Backend Support for Product Cube:

• Completed internationalization adaptation, copy fixes, and resource additions for the Product Cube landing page.

• Smoothly migrated the requirement management module to be handled uniformly by Product Cube.
🛠️ Under the Hood

• Log & Monitoring Optimization: Hid redundant logs for Apple server callback interfaces and data tracking reporting interfaces. Fixed errors in tracking reporting and corrected the log level for missing email sending templates, keeping the logging system clean.

• Fixed a bug where types were accidentally modified when updating configurations.

🌍 Easy I18n: Editor Experience & Build Upgrade
The internationalization entry management tool, Easy I18n, focused this week on improving developer editing efficiency and completed an underlying build version upgrade.
📊 Weekly Overview

• Features Delivered: 8
🚀 Core Highlights
⚡ Build Upgrade:
Upgraded the local build script compilation version to 1.6.0 and added support for multi-language entries.
✍️ Entry Editor Experience Optimization:

• Added more text shortcut operations to the term editing dialog. The “More” button at the bottom has been refactored and equipped with intuitive icons.

• Fixed an issue where the copy button at the end was obscured and unclickable when the copy text for the top entry was too long, ensuring UI robustness across various extreme text lengths.

🍃 Leaf Note (iOS): Visual Fix & Community Engagement
The “Leaf Note” iOS client focused primarily on rapidly responding to community feedback this week, while maintaining a high frequency of social media content output.
📊 Weekly Overview

• Feedback Handled: 2

• Features Delivered: 1

• Content Operations: 5 social media posts
🛠️ Fixes & Optimizations
🖼️ Core Bug Fix:
Completely fixed the visual bug where note images were not displaying, restoring the complete reading experience for image-and-text layouts.

📈 Operations & Community
Beyond code-level iterations, we also maintained extremely high activity in community operations this week:

• Product Cube published 3 social media posts, continuously warming up for the ProductHunt launch.

• Leaf Note published 5 social media posts and actively handled 2 user feedback items, maintaining a close connection between the product and its users.

💡 Takeaways
This week’s R&D work not only covered a comprehensive blooming from underlying architecture (such as Git dependency removal, Apple payment timing fixes) to frontend visuals (Product Cube homepage revamp, backend platform UI upgrades), but also took a solid step forward in the business implementation of AI capabilities.
Next week, we will continue to advance the ProductHunt launch preparations for “Product Cube” and continuously collect user feedback to polish product details.
Stay tuned, and happy coding! 🚀

If you enjoyed this development weekly report, feel free to clap and follow. For any questions or suggestions, please leave a comment below!
